steps.title: Read the context
steps.content: Nature seemed to put "every <u>conceivable</u> obstacle" in the way — the writer emphasizes the sheer difficulty of crossing the Isthmus of Panama by invoking every possible kind of obstacle one could think of.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The underlined word needs to mean "possible to think of" — every obstacle the mind could imagine. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Imaginable: "every imaginable obstacle" — imaginable means capable of being thought of or pictured, a precise match for "every possible kind." ✓
  • Choice B — Obvious: "every obvious obstacle" — obvious means plainly visible or evident; this limits obstacles to only those easy to see, not every possible one. ✗
  • Choice C — Reasonable: "every reasonable obstacle" — reasonable means sensible or logical; this limits obstacles to only those that make sense, missing the sense of overwhelming totality. ✗
  • Choice D — Dependable: "every dependable obstacle" — dependable means reliable or trustworthy; obstacles aren't characterized by reliability in this context. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
